rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions to avoid '<error>' type mismatches.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference non-existent or inaccessible features.
generic_rules: Validate all AGREE expressions against visible AADL types and features.
Only reference identifiers that are explicitly declared or visible in the current scope.
Avoid generating code that introduces new package dependencies or data types.
Remove invalid AGREE constructs rather than attempting to patch them with new architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only int, real, and bool as scalar types in AGREE const and eq declarations.
Left-hand sides of assignments in implementation scope must be outputs or declared variables.
Guarantees in component type annexes must reference visible ports, not subcomponent paths.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations.
Do not mix implementation-level subcomponent references in component type contracts.
Do not assume event ports have numeric or Boolean encodings without explicit model support.
Do not generate AGREE code that introduces new package or data type dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
